smrfdplobs7vz.text:
• Inter Milan finalized the acquisition of Anan Khalaili on July 10, 2026. The Nerazzurri will pay €25M plus bonuses to secure the player. This total package reaches approximately €30M including a small resale clause. The 21-year-old fullback has already arrived in Italy to finalize the deal.

• Majdi, the father of Anan Khalaili, spoke to Haaretz regarding the significant transfer. He stated, "It's the biggest move in the history of Israeli football." The player previously held status as the number one, two, and three candidate after Marco Palestra joined Chelsea. Khalaili maintains a long-term dream of playing for Liverpool.

• The 21-year-old player spent the last two seasons in Belgium playing for Union SG. He recorded 8 goals and 11 assists during 99 appearances for his former team. His career began at Maccabi Haifa before moving to the Belgian league.

• Real Madrid activated a €20M release clause to poach Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan. The club needed a replacement following this unexpected loss of the fullback. Inter Milan had previously pursued Marco Palestra before shifting focus to Khalaili. This recruitment strategy prioritizes young talent over more established names.

• Anan Khalaili will sign a five-year contract with the Italian club. He is set to earn €2.2M per season under the new agreement. Analysts are monitoring if Inter Milan will consider signing Fiorentina's Dodo to provide stability. The club must decide whether to trust the newcomer or sell Luis Henrique.

smrfeoy51sx21.text:
Bristol City have signed defender Lisav Eissat on a four-year deal for an undisclosed fee, subject to international clearance and visa approval.
Able to operate as a centre-back and a left-back, the 21-year-old joins City on a deal until 2030.
Eissat is City’s fifth signing of the summer and boosts Michael Skubala’s options in defence.
On adding to the group ahead of City’s training camp in Spain, First Team Manager Skubala said: “It is really pleasing to bolster our defence, especially with a player who can play more than one position.
“This experience of English football will be good for him.”
The defender made his senior international debut for Romania in October 2025 and currently has four caps to his name.
Eissat said on his first day at City: “I am looking forward to it. I have followed the Championship and Premier League football a lot and I like the intensity and the style, which is like my playing style.
“I am an aggressive and fast defender and ready to jump in. I am ready to give everything for the club.”
Sporting Director James Ellis added: “Bringing Lisav to Bristol City is to help us improve squad depth, versatility, physicality and potential of the back line.
“We felt in the squad build it was important we addressed the challenges the group had last year, especially with robustness and availability.
“The management, coaching and support team are looking forward to working with him and hope the fans and community connect with him early on.”
*Bristol City*
Share
